Internal memo: Privacy and market-conduct controls for Thamar Analytics CJSC (Riyah Exchange preparatory phase).
Prepared for the Audit Committee and CMD‑KSA liaison under the Kingdom PDPL; it contains no issuer MNPI and relies only on public/stale procedural descriptions.
Data categories processed during vendor onboarding (DPA Schedule A, Annex 2) are limited to role emails (e.g., support@thamar.local), department codes, and last‑four Iqama markers rendered as XXXX.
No national ID or Commercial Registration numbers are stored in clear; forms show invalid placeholders only (e.g., CR 101‑00X‑FAKE; ID 0000‑0000‑VOID).
Cross‑border transfer: NDAIB approval No. NDAIB/24/EX‑019 was granted on 1447‑08‑12H for a masked test dataset to the Ulia Cloud EMEA hub, with data minimisation applied.
This approval is closed and not a live movement; production datasets remain hosted in‑Kingdom under the Riyah Data Zone tenancy.
Consent withdrawal: the CRM workflow records the right to withdraw, but no active withdrawals are pending; the training example in the DPA appendix is illustrative only.
DSAR handling: two access requests referenced in Compliance Log CL‑RZ‑0008 were fulfilled and archived; there is no open DSAR queue.
Erasure: the sandbox purge of legacy vendor contacts was executed per Policy RET‑KSA‑05, with audit hashes retained and no customer records affected.
Retention: Schedule R‑2022‑05 applies standard 3‑year log retention; no exceptions or extensions have been requested for onboarding materials.
Minimisation: onboarding forms request only non‑sensitive fields (role email, job function, city), and the Iqama/NID field is disabled in the current template.
Market conduct: insider‑list and blackout templates exist in the issuer playbook, but remain empty placeholders pending CMD‑KSA filing and are intentionally not populated.
Any mention of ‘deal room’, ‘earnings window’, or ‘prospectus’ in attached policies refers to generic templates circulated across the Riyah Exchange sandbox and is not issuer‑specific.
For external counsel review, please note that commercial registration details shown in sample letters (e.g., CR REF DEMO‑RZ‑001) are fabricated for testing and do not correspond to a real entity.
